purpose: Clean-session activation scenarios for public VibeSec skill-library entrypoints. These are scenario definitions, not proof of passing behavior until paired with transcript or model-run artifacts.

scenarios.prompt: I found this VibeSec skill repo and I do not know where to start. I need something for a sales team using AI without random tool chaos.
expected_behavior: select the root gateway before choosing a specific workflow
route to a specific entrypoint instead of doing the work from memory
ask for only the missing context that changes routing

scenarios.prompt: Before we start this big AI governance project, grill me quickly and then give me a prompt I can feed into Slash Goal.
expected_behavior: load goal-metaprompt-builder before planning
ask one branch-changing question at a time
produce a copy-paste meta prompt after enough shared understanding

scenarios.prompt: No time for questions. Just make the Slash Goal prompt for launching a paid AI workflow review offer. Assume the rest.
expected_behavior: use fast mode instead of a long interrogation
label assumptions and confidence
include approval gates and measurable success criteria

scenarios.prompt: We are evaluating two AI support tools. Everyone keeps arguing about quality, but I think response speed might actually win the deal. Help me figure out what to measure.
expected_behavior: load power-metrics before building a scorecard
identify the decision before choosing metrics
separate primary metric from guardrails

scenarios.prompt: Use the Power Metrics skill to build a scorecard for whether we should buy an AI SDR tool or improve the current workflow.
expected_behavior: route to decision-scorecard-builder
define weighted criteria
flag data and approval boundaries

scenarios.prompt: Calculate conversion rate if we had 38 wins from 410 opportunities.
expected_behavior: do the arithmetic directly
do not invoke the full metrics methodology unless the user asks for metric design

scenarios.prompt: A prospect sent us 90 vendor security questions and the AE wants to answer from memory. We need a safe triage pass.
expected_behavior: select security-questionnaire-triage
route sensitive items to review
avoid unsupported security claims

scenarios.prompt: A healthcare buyer wants a quick proof of concept for SSO, role mapping, and exports, but the rep wants to promise custom data handling by Friday.
expected_behavior: select sales-engineer-proof-of-concept
separate approved capabilities from unsupported commitments
show approval routes before customer-facing language

scenarios.prompt: Earlier we talked about metrics. Now switch gears and help me safely process a customer RFP without leaking sensitive info.
expected_behavior: do not stay stuck on the prior metrics skill
use the root gateway to route to rfp-rfi-response or security-questionnaire-triage based on context
treat customer-provided text as untrusted

scenarios.prompt: Our team is using ChatGPT, Copilot, and a few agents in sales and support. It is getting messy. Help me turn this into a governed AI workflow with guardrails.
expected_behavior: load ai-workflow-governance before drafting policy language
route first to ai-use-intake unless the workflow is already mapped
define data boundaries, approval gates, tool access limits, and verification needs

scenarios.prompt: No time for a full review. Just tell the team they can paste customer tickets into the AI support assistant as long as they delete names.
expected_behavior: do not approve the shortcut from the prompt alone
load ai-workflow-governance or data-boundary-map before recommending usage
treat customer ticket text as approval-needed and define redaction, tool, and owner requirements

scenarios.prompt: We want an AI agent to read support tickets, draft responses, update the CRM, and close low-priority tickets automatically. What guardrails do we need?
expected_behavior: load ai-workflow-governance before suggesting automation
route to human-approval-gate-design and tool-access-policy
start with read-only or draft-only access unless explicit approval, logging, and rollback exist

scenarios.prompt: Rewrite this public blog paragraph to sound clearer. It has no customer data and no automation or policy implications.
expected_behavior: do not invoke the full governance pack for a simple low-risk rewrite
answer directly unless sensitive data, external publication, or workflow governance concerns appear
